content.problemStatement:
Chef has  NNN boxes arranged in a line.Each box has some candies in it,say C[i]C[i]C[i]. Chef wants to distribute all the candies between of his friends: AAA and BBB, in the following way:

AAA starts eating candies kept in the leftmost box(box at 111st place) and BBB starts eating candies kept in the rightmost box(box at NNNth place). AAA eats  candies XXX times faster than BBB i.e. AAA eats XXX candies when BBB eats only 111. Candies in each box can be eaten by only the person who reaches that box first. If both reach a box at the same time, then the one who has eaten from more number of BOXES till now will eat the candies in that box. If both have eaten from the same number of boxes till now ,then AAA will eat the candies in that box.
Find the number of boxes finished by both AAA and BBB.

**NOTE:-** We assume that it does not take any time to switch from one box to another.

###Input:


First line will contain TTT, number of testcases. Then the testcases follow.
Each testcase contains three lines of input.
The first line of each test case contains NNN, the number of boxes.
The second line of each test case contains a sequence,C1C_1C1​ ,C2C_2C2​ ,C3C_3C3​ . . . CNC_NCN​ where CiC_iCi​ denotes the number of candies in the i-th box.
The third line of each test case contains an integer XXX .
###Output:
For each testcase, print two space separated integers in a new line - the number of boxes eaten by AAA  and  BBB respectively.

###Constraints


1≤T≤1001 \leq T \leq 1001≤T≤100
1≤N≤2000001 \leq N \leq 2000001≤N≤200000
1≤Ci≤10000001 \leq C_i \leq 10000001≤Ci​≤1000000
1≤X≤101 \leq X \leq 101≤X≤10
Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 300000300000300000
###Subtasks
Subtask 1(24 points):


1≤T≤101 \leq T \leq 101≤T≤10
1≤N≤10001 \leq N \leq 10001≤N≤1000
1≤Ci≤10001 \leq C_i \leq 10001≤Ci​≤1000
1≤X≤101 \leq X \leq 101≤X≤10
Subtask 2(51 points): original constraints

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
AAA will start eating candies in the 1st box(leftmost box having 2 candies) . BBB will start eating candies in the  5th box(rightmost box having 9 candies) .As A′sA'sA′s speed is 2 times as that of B′sB'sB′s, AAA will start eating candies in the  2nd box while BBB would still be eating candies in the 5th box.AAA will now start eating candies from the 3rd box while BBB would still be eating candies in the 5th box.Now both AAA and BBB will finish candies in their respective boxes at the same time(AAA from the 3rd box and BBB from 5th box). Since AAA has eaten more number of boxes(AAA has eaten 3 boxes while BBB has eaten 1 box) till now,so AAA will be eating candies in the 4th box.
Hence AAA has eaten 4 boxes and BBB has eaten 1 box.
